summary:
The patient likely has Vitamin K Deficiency due to proportionately prolonged PT/APTT without changes in platelets/fibrinogens; responsiveness to IV vitamin K further supports this diagnosis as it rapidly corrects the clotting times indicative specific pathophysiology unique just among true cases of VLD leading us conclusively towards option 'D'. To reiterate succinctly:

The patient most probably suffers from Vitamin K Deficiency evidenced by consistent lab findings including normalized test results after administration of vitamin k indicating its role directly linked back etiologically making other possibilities less plausible compared against current data set provided thus concluding confidently at point ‘D’.
